meaning: vt.改编成为戏剧,编写剧本vi.戏剧地表现
examples: The film attempts neither to dramatize nor sermonize.
The Harvard Business Review printed a lively, fictional exchange of letters to dramatize complaints about business degree holders.
They have a tendency to show off, to dramatize almost every situation.
Figuring how to place and dramatize seven individual people was one of the painter's problems.
The Nobelists wanted to dramatize the importance of the EU record at a time of great challenge.
